---
title: "Configuration"
---

All settings live under the `nowPlaying.*` namespace in VSCode's settings.

| Setting                              | Default                              | Description |
|--------------------------------------|--------------------------------------|-------------|
| `nowPlaying.format`                  | `{playerIcon} {artist} - {title}`    | Status bar format. See *Format placeholders* below. |
| `nowPlaying.maxLength`               | `50`                                 | Max characters before truncation with ellipsis (or marquee scrolling, if enabled). |
| `nowPlaying.alignment`               | `right`                              | Status bar alignment (`left` or `right`). |
| `nowPlaying.priority`                | `100`                                | Status bar priority (higher = further from edges). |
| `nowPlaying.showControls`            | `true`                               | Show separate prev/play-pause/next status bar buttons. |
| `nowPlaying.preferredPlayer`         | `""`                                 | Preferred MPRIS player suffix (e.g. `spotify`). Empty = first available. |
| `nowPlaying.sidecarPath`             | `""`                                 | Path to the `now-playing` binary. Empty = use bundled binary. |
| `nowPlaying.hidePausedAfterSeconds`  | `0`                                  | Hide the bar after the player has been paused this many seconds. `0` disables. |
| `nowPlaying.hideIdleAfterSeconds`    | `0`                                  | Hide the bar after the player has been stopped/idle this many seconds. `0` disables. |
| `nowPlaying.formatRules`             | `[]`                                 | Ordered overrides. Each rule is `{ when: { field: regex, ... }, format: "..." }`. First match wins. |
| `nowPlaying.playerIcons`             | `{}`                                 | Override or extend the built-in `{playerIcon}` map. Keys are MPRIS bus suffixes; values are codicon strings. |
| `nowPlaying.marquee.enabled`         | `false`                              | Scroll the status bar text as a marquee when it exceeds `maxLength`, instead of truncating. |
| `nowPlaying.marquee.speedMs`         | `300`                                | Milliseconds between marquee shifts (lower = faster). |
| `nowPlaying.marquee.pauseEndsMs`     | `1500`                               | Milliseconds to pause when the marquee wraps back to the start. |
| `nowPlaying.marquee.gap`             | `"   "`                              | Separator inserted between end and start of the marquee loop. |

## Format placeholders

The `nowPlaying.format` string is rendered with these substitutions:

| Placeholder    | Value                                                            |
|----------------|------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `{playerIcon}` | Codicon for the active MPRIS player (see *Player icons* below)   |
| `{artist}`     | Track artist                                                     |
| `{title}`      | Track title                                                      |
| `{album}`      | Album name                                                       |
| `{position}`   | Current playback position, `mm:ss` (or `h:mm:ss` over one hour)  |
| `{length}`     | Track duration, same format as `{position}`                      |
| `{player}`     | MPRIS player identifier (e.g. `spotify`)                         |
| `{status}`     | `playing`, `paused`, `stopped`, or `none`                        |

Missing fields render as empty and adjacent ` - ` separators collapse, so a
format like `"{playerIcon} {artist} - {title} [{position}/{length}]"` degrades
gracefully when a player does not expose position or album metadata.

You can also embed VSCode codicons via the `$(icon-name)` syntax:

```json
{
  "nowPlaying.format": "$(unmute) {artist} - {title}",
  "nowPlaying.maxLength": 60
}
```

## Per-media overrides

`nowPlaying.formatRules` lets you swap the template for specific media. Each
rule's `when` block is a field-to-regex map (case-insensitive); all listed
fields must match. Matchable fields: `title`, `artist`, `album`, `player`,
`status`.

```json
{
  "nowPlaying.formatRules": [
    { "when": { "title": "Netflix" }, "format": "$(device-camera-video) Netflix" },
    { "when": { "player": "spotify" }, "format": "$(music) {artist} - {title}" }
  ]
}
```

## Player icons

`{playerIcon}` resolves via a merged map (built-in defaults + the user
setting `nowPlaying.playerIcons`). Keys are the MPRIS bus suffix
(`state.player`); values are codicon strings. Players that expose suffixes
like `firefox.instance_1_84` fall back to the prefix before the first dot.

```json
{
  "nowPlaying.playerIcons": {
    "spotify": "$(megaphone)",
    "amberol": "$(music)"
  }
}
```

## Marquee scrolling

When the rendered status bar text is longer than `nowPlaying.maxLength`, the
default behavior is to truncate with an ellipsis. Set
`nowPlaying.marquee.enabled` to `true` to scroll the text instead:

```json
{
  "nowPlaying.marquee.enabled": true,
  "nowPlaying.marquee.speedMs": 250,
  "nowPlaying.marquee.pauseEndsMs": 2000,
  "nowPlaying.marquee.gap": "   ***   "
}
```

`gap` is the visible separator stitched between the end of the text and the
start when it loops; `pauseEndsMs` adds a short pause each time the marquee
wraps so the beginning is readable.

## Hover tooltip

Hovering the status bar item shows a richer view: the cover art (when the
player exposes one), the track title, and the album with its release year in
parentheses when available (e.g. *Random Access Memories (2013)*). No
configuration is needed.

## Picking a specific player

If multiple MPRIS players are running, set the preferred one by the suffix of
its bus name (the part after `org.mpris.MediaPlayer2.`):

```json
{
  "nowPlaying.preferredPlayer": "spotify"
}
```

List currently exposed players:

```bash
busctl --user list | grep mpris
```
